findings.message: forge.toml missing [platform] block

advocacy.why: per VERSION_DISCIPLINE.md, every site declares its pinned forge_version + loom_version + crawler_version so upgrades are explicit, not silent
advocacy.substrate_fix:
add `[platform]
forge_version = "<exact>"
loom_version = "<exact>"
crawler_version = "<exact>"` to forge.toml

findings.message: cms page missing `version` field

advocacy.why: VERSION_DISCIPLINE.md § Per-artifact-class versioning: every CMS page carries a `version` field; absence prevents auto-migration when the page schema evolves
advocacy.substrate_fix: add `"version": "1.0.0"` to the page's top-level JSON object

findings.message: 20.5K HTML > 20.0K budget — audit blocks / split route

advocacy.why: every kilobyte over budget is one more TLS round-trip the reader's browser has to wait for before paint — the budget is the reader-side latency floor, not an internal lint
advocacy.substrate_fix: HTML over budget at 20.5K: audit blocks / split route
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't raise the budget to silence the warn — every active substrate uses the same defaults so over-budget assets are a real regression, not a config mismatch

findings.message: [cta-contact] declared but impl_files is empty (PARTIAL — stub)

advocacy.why: a backend with empty impl_files is a partial declaration; phantom_button passes (the slug exists) but at runtime the backend has nowhere to dispatch to
advocacy.substrate_fix: populate impl_files for [cta-contact] with the Rust module path(s) that handle the backend's verbs — OR remove the declaration if the implementation isn't planned

findings.message: href="/prosper-in-life/" → static/prosper-in-life/ does not exist

advocacy.why: every internal href in shipped HTML must resolve to a file in static/. an unresolved href ships to readers as a 404 — silent in the build, loud in the browser
advocacy.substrate_fix: either: (a) add cms/prosper-in-life/.json so render writes static/prosper-in-life/, OR (b) remove the dead link from the rendering source (CMS body / Loom primitive props)
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't manually `touch static/<route>.html` to make the gate pass — that file is regenerated only from cms/*.json on write_canonical builds and will be flagged as an orphan

image_desert: page has 16 content section(s) and zero image / icon / illustration / logo references — the page feels uninhabited; consider adding visual texture via feature_spotlight icons, logo_cloud, picture, or image_hero with photo background

advocacy.why: a page that's all text reads as either (a) a legal disclosure where text density is the point or (b) an unfinished page where the editor forgot the visual layer; the detector can't distinguish, so it flags both and lets the editor decide
advocacy.substrate_fix: if this is genuinely text-only by intent (privacy / terms / disclosure), add a single illustrative section above the body (an image_hero with a 'compact' or 'narrow' height, an asset_slug pointer to a relevant icon, or a feature_spotlight with one item carrying an icon_slug). If this is unfinished, add 2-3 visual sections to break up the wall of text
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't stuff a stock photo onto a legal page just to silence this gate — the gate exists to flag uninhabited pages, not to require photos on every page. A single icon next to the title is enough visual anchor for a disclosure page

image_hero.lede contains marketing phrase "purpose-built" (within "Purpose-built for planning and building products with AI agents. Linear is shaped by the practices the highest-throughput product teams converged on independently."). The substring is unambiguous SaaS-marketing slop — rewrite the sentence to drop it.

advocacy.why: phrases like 'ai-native', 'industry-leading', 'next-generation' are marketing modifiers that carry no editorial substance; their presence anywhere is a tell that the copy was generated rather than written.
advocacy.substrate_fix: remove the phrase "purpose-built" and restate what the sentence is actually about. If you can't restate it without the cliche, the sentence has no content to restate — drop the field.
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't rewrite to a synonym from the same family ('cutting-edge' → 'state-of-the-art') — the family is the problem.

findings.message: dev server not responding — start it (e.g. `python3 -m http.server 8123 --directory static`) and re-run; runtime audit skipped

advocacy.why: crawl probes the dev server via raw TCP connect to 127.0.0.1:8123 before invoking the crawler — prevents the cascade-of-fake-regressions when the server is silently down (every axis reports failure for the wrong reason)
advocacy.substrate_fix: start a static file server at 127.0.0.1:8123 pointing at static/ (e.g. `python3 -m http.server 8123 --directory static`, or any equivalent that serves the freshly rendered tree), then re-run forge build
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't bind the dev server to a different port without updating the probe — crawl is hard-pinned to 8123 because the journey URLs hard-code it
